mame-software-lists.name: MAME Software Lists
mame-software-lists.notes: MAME software-list metadata for computers/consoles and non-arcade machines.

playnite-metadata-plugins.name: Playnite Metadata Plugins
playnite-metadata-plugins.notes: Playnite metadata plugins and library integrations.

rawg-provider.name: RAWG Provider
rawg-provider.notes: General game metadata provider; matching quality varies for ROM collections.

redump-dat.name: Redump DATs
redump-dat.notes: Disc preservation DAT source and naming/hashing reference.

retroachievements-import.name: RetroAchievements Metadata
retroachievements-import.notes: Achievement metadata/source awareness; not a replacement for ROM metadata scrapers.

retrobat-built-in-scraper.name: RetroBat Built-in Scraper
retrobat-built-in-scraper.notes: RetroBat/EmulationStation scraper behavior around gamelist.xml and downloaded media.

romm-metadata-providers.name: RomM Metadata Providers
romm-metadata-providers.notes: RomM enrichment providers used during server-side library scans.

skraper.name: Skraper
skraper.notes: Windows GUI scraper, free with a ScreenScraper account. Very popular for RetroBat and Batocera setups.

skyscraper.name: Skyscraper
skyscraper.notes: Powerful CLI scraper with local caching. Preferred for Batocera and headless Linux setups. Supports Pegasus metadata format.

sscraper.name: sselph/scraper
sscraper.notes: Lightweight CLI scraper. Fast and good for automated or headless setups.

universal-xml-scraper.name: Universal XML Scraper
universal-xml-scraper.notes: Legacy ScreenScraper-oriented XML/media scraper still found in older ES workflows.

7z-stdin-multidisc.description: When invoking 7-Zip inside a bash while-read loop that reads from a file (done < file), 7-Zip reads from and consumes the loop's stdin, causing all subsequent loop iterations to receive empty input. Only the first archive is extracted.
7z-stdin-multidisc.symptom: After running an extraction loop over a list of archives, only the first archive (Disc 1) is extracted for every multi-disc game. The loop processes fewer items than expected and exits early.
7z-stdin-multidisc.fix: Redirect stdin to /dev/null on every 7-Zip invocation inside loops: '"$SEVENZIP" e "$archive" -o"$dest" -y < /dev/null'

android-scoped-storage.description: Android frontends operate under scoped storage and SAF permission rules.
android-scoped-storage.symptom: A frontend sees folders during setup but cannot launch, scrape, or write media later.
android-scoped-storage.fix: Verify storage permissions, document tree URIs, and app-specific media directories before changing paths.

es-folder-alias-mismatch.description: ES-family frontends use frontend-specific folder IDs for the same platform.
es-folder-alias-mismatch.symptom: A library looks complete but games do not appear because files are in genesis while the frontend expects megadrive, or ds while it expects nds.
es-folder-alias-mismatch.fix: Resolve the active frontend folder map before moving files; prefer adding aliases to user.json over bulk renames until confirmed.

es-loose-images.description: EmulationStation scans subdirectories and detects loose disc image files (.cue, .bin, .gdi) alongside .m3u playlist files, causing each disc to appear as a separate game entry in addition to the .m3u entry.
es-loose-images.symptom: Games appear multiple times in the frontend — once for the .m3u and once per disc file. Multi-disc games show individual disc entries alongside the combined .m3u entry.
es-loose-images.fix: Move all disc image subfolders to a sibling directory outside the system ROM folder (e.g. sonyplaystation/images/ instead of sonyplaystation/Favorite/). Update .m3u files to use relative paths pointing to the new location (e.g. ..\images\GameName\disc.cue on Windows/RetroBat).

es-m3u-folder-name.description: EmulationStation-based frontends (RetroBat, Batocera, ES-DE) do not recognize a directory named with an .m3u extension (e.g. 'Game Title.m3u/') as a game. Only actual .m3u files at the system ROM folder root are detected.
es-m3u-folder-name.symptom: Games stored in folders named '<game>.m3u' do not appear in the frontend at all, even if the folder contains valid disc images and an inner .m3u file.
es-m3u-folder-name.fix: Rename the directory to remove the .m3u extension. Create a proper .m3u playlist file at the system ROM folder root referencing the disc images with correct relative paths.

find-dir-name-match.description: The find command matches both files AND directories by name pattern. When directories are named with file extensions (e.g. 'Game.m3u/'), 'find . -name "*.m3u"' matches the directory itself in addition to any .m3u files inside.
find-dir-name-match.symptom: Scripts that search for .m3u files operate on directories instead of files, producing empty content reads, wrong path handling, or silent errors.
find-dir-name-match.fix: Always add '-type f' when targeting files: 'find "$dir" -type f -name "*.m3u"'. Add '-mindepth 1' as well when scanning inside a folder named with the same extension.

launchbox-native-multidisc.description: LaunchBox does not require .m3u playlists for multi-disc games. It tracks additional discs through its own game metadata and Additional Apps entries.
launchbox-native-multidisc.symptom: Imported .m3u files may appear as separate games or fail to map cleanly to LaunchBox's database-driven multi-disc model.
launchbox-native-multidisc.fix: Prefer LaunchBox's built-in multi-disc handling. Add Disc 2+ as Additional Apps or use LaunchBox import tools rather than converting every multi-disc set to .m3u.

launchbox-path-rewrite.description: LaunchBox XML stores absolute and relative paths that can break after drive moves.
launchbox-path-rewrite.symptom: Games or media disappear after moving the LaunchBox folder or ROM drive.
launchbox-path-rewrite.fix: Back up Data/Platforms XML files, then use LaunchBox path repair/import tools or scripted path rewrites with validation.

mame-chd-parent-folder.description: MAME CHDs usually belong in a folder named after the parent ROM ZIP.
mame-chd-parent-folder.symptom: Arcade games with CHDs fail even though both ZIP and CHD files exist.
mame-chd-parent-folder.fix: Keep parent ZIP at the system root and CHDs under a same-named subfolder; do not flatten CHDs blindly.

mame-romset-version.description: MAME and FBNeo ROM sets are version-sensitive. A ZIP that works with one MAME or FBNeo version may be missing files for another version.
mame-romset-version.symptom: Arcade games fail to launch even though the ZIP exists. Logs mention missing ROMs, wrong checksums, missing BIOS, or missing parent sets.
mame-romset-version.fix: Identify the target emulator/core version, then audit against a matching full/non-merged/split ROM set. Do not unzip arcade ROMs as a repair step.

mame-split-merged-confusion.description: Split, merged, and non-merged arcade sets have different parent/clone/BIOS expectations.
mame-split-merged-confusion.symptom: Only some clones launch; logs report missing parent or BIOS files.
mame-split-merged-confusion.fix: Identify set type and emulator version before rebuilding; avoid deleting parent/BIOS ZIPs as duplicates.

multidisc-chd-m3u-edge-cases.description: Multi-disc CHD and .m3u support differs across cores and standalone emulators.
multidisc-chd-m3u-edge-cases.symptom: A playlist resolves but disc swapping fails, or the emulator opens only disc 1.
multidisc-chd-m3u-edge-cases.fix: Check emulator-specific .m3u syntax and whether CHD is supported directly; verify with a sample before bulk conversion.

mv-same-name-dir.description: On Linux, 'mv source dest' moves source INSIDE dest (rather than renaming it) when dest already exists as a directory. This creates an unexpected nested structure.
mv-same-name-dir.symptom: 'mv "Game.m3u" "Game"' when a Game/ directory already exists creates Game/Game.m3u/ nested inside Game/, rather than renaming the folder. Subsequent writes to Game.m3u (as a file) may fail or produce unexpected results.
mv-same-name-dir.fix: Check for the existence of the destination directory before renaming: 'if [ -d "$dest" ]; then handle_conflict; else mv "$src" "$dest"; fi'

no-intro-redump-name-vs-frontend-name.description: Preservation DAT names can be poor display names for frontends.
no-intro-redump-name-vs-frontend-name.symptom: Frontend titles include long region/revision metadata or fail friendly-name matching.
no-intro-redump-name-vs-frontend-name.fix: Keep DAT names for validation where needed, but use metadata display names for frontend presentation.

parser-glob-collision.description: Parser rules can match playlists, raw discs, shortcuts, and subfolders at the same time.
parser-glob-collision.symptom: Duplicate entries appear in Steam, HyperSpin, Attract-Mode, or other parser-driven frontends.
parser-glob-collision.fix: Audit parser globs and exclude raw disc extensions when .m3u or shortcut files are intended launch targets.

playnite-emulator-profile-paths.description: Playnite emulator profiles and library plugins can hold stale executable and ROM paths.
playnite-emulator-profile-paths.symptom: Games exist but launch with missing emulator or file-not-found errors.
playnite-emulator-profile-paths.fix: Audit emulator profiles, install directories, and library plugin settings before editing game records.

retroarch-system-dir-confusion.description: RetroArch System/BIOS directory may differ from frontend BIOS folders.
retroarch-system-dir-confusion.symptom: A game launches from standalone emulator but fails from RetroArch core with missing BIOS.
retroarch-system-dir-confusion.fix: Check RetroArch Directory > System/BIOS setting and core docs; do not assume frontend bios/ is active.

romm-case-sensitive-scan.description: Server-side Linux scans are case-sensitive even when source collections came from Windows.
romm-case-sensitive-scan.symptom: Media or ROM files fail to match on Linux despite matching visually on Windows.
romm-case-sensitive-scan.fix: Normalize or record exact case and avoid case-only renames without a two-step move on case-insensitive filesystems.

romm-platform-slug-mismatch.description: RomM platform slugs and folder names must map to supported/custom platforms.
romm-platform-slug-mismatch.symptom: Server scan imports ROMs under Unknown or skips expected metadata enrichment.
romm-platform-slug-mismatch.fix: Check RomM supported platform slugs and custom platform config before renaming large folders.

scraper-false-region-match.description: Scrapers may false-match region, revision, hack, beta, translated, or clone ROM names.
scraper-false-region-match.symptom: Artwork/title/description belongs to a different region or game variant.
scraper-false-region-match.fix: Produce a mismatch report and ask for confirmation before rewriting metadata.

steam-rom-manager-duplicates.description: EmuDeck libraries may be surfaced both through ES-DE and Steam ROM Manager. Parser rules can create duplicate Steam entries if both playlist files and raw image files match a parser.
steam-rom-manager-duplicates.symptom: Steam shows duplicate games or individual disc entries after parsing, even when ES-DE looks correct.
steam-rom-manager-duplicates.fix: Check the active Steam ROM Manager parser and exclude hidden image folders or raw disc extensions when .m3u files are the intended launch targets.

steamgrid-artwork-overwrite.description: Steam ROM Manager can overwrite custom artwork during parser regeneration.
steamgrid-artwork-overwrite.symptom: Custom grid/hero/logo assets disappear after re-parsing.
steamgrid-artwork-overwrite.fix: Back up artwork and disable artwork overwrite or lock custom artwork before parser regeneration.

switch-keys-secrets.description: Switch emulator setups require user-dumped keys and firmware, but these are sensitive deployment artifacts and should not be stored in rom-librarian user.json or committed to the repository.
switch-keys-secrets.symptom: Emulator reports missing prod.keys, title.keys, firmware, or encrypted content errors.
switch-keys-secrets.fix: Tell the user which files are missing and where the emulator expects them, but never ask the user to paste keys into chat or save keys in rom-librarian configs.

wsl-path-7zip.description: Windows 7-Zip (7z.exe) cannot handle Linux/WSL-style paths (/mnt/g/...). It requires native Windows paths with drive letters and backslashes (G:\games\...).
wsl-path-7zip.symptom: 7z.exe called from WSL bash with /mnt/... paths fails with 'cannot open file' or 'no such file' errors.
wsl-path-7zip.fix: Convert paths with wslpath before passing to 7z.exe: 'win_path=$(wslpath -w "$linux_path")'. Apply to both the archive path and the output destination path.
